public_reputation: Known around Hullwake Foundry through day-to-day work as commerce broker.
appearance: Mirelle Tideglass carries the marks of commerce broker in ink, rain, and polished cloth: practical wear, maintained details, and a look that suggests they expect to be read before they are trusted.
manner: Evaluates every conversation for margin, dependency, and what can be made to move next. In Hullwake Foundry, that habit reads through the public pressures of the place rather than decorative flourish.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of commerce broker in and around Hullwake Foundry, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Protect their footing in Hullwake Foundry while steering current strain away from a public rupture they may not survive politically or materially.
hidden_pressure: A private obligation, debt, promise, or fear tied to reputational exposure is narrowing their options faster than they admit.

adventure_hooks.summary: Mirelle Tideglass can draw the party into a locality problem centered on commerce broker duties in Hullwake Foundry.
Mirelle Tideglass becomes useful when the party needs grounded help with supply bottlenecks, labor fatigue around Hullwake Foundry.

support_scope_notes: Created in V33_1 to ensure every locality-facing NPC access role resolves to a proper named person while remaining sparse and canon-safe.
public_signal: In public, Mirelle Tideglass is immediately readable as a commerce broker tied to Hullwake Foundry.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Hullwake Foundry: ordinary workspaces, public counters, roads, docks, shrines, workshops, or other role-relevant spaces
who_seeks_them_out: people who need access or clarity

first_impression: Mirelle Tideglass presents as someone shaped by the duties of a commerce broker and the daily pressures of Hullwake Foundry.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for the work of a commerce broker, and proof that strangers will not make Hullwake Foundry harder to manage.
what_they_refuse: Mirelle Tideglass refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing the expectations of a commerce broker too rigidly, protecting local reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Can redirect supply, access networks, and read market pressure clearly.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Mirelle Tideglass adds scrutiny, delay, and locality-specific friction before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Mirelle Tideglass always knows more about Hullwake Foundry than is immediately visible.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or relief tied to a commerce broker in Hullwake Foundry.
social_texture: Mirelle Tideglass speaks like someone who has spent too long translating private stakes into acceptable public language. Around Hullwake Foundry, people read them as socially alert, procedural, and sensitive to status.
money_pressure: Their material pressure is tied to favors and tariffs; even when wealthy, they think in terms of loss, leverage, and what one bad week would expose.
status_anxiety: What they fear most is not simple failure but reputational exposure becoming socially visible before they can contain it.
long_memory: They remember who made their work harder, who preserved dignity under strain, and who tried to force speed without bearing consequence.
obvious_tell: When pressure rises, Mirelle touches clothing, tools, or insignia before answering, as if checking that their role is still intact.
best_use_case: Use Mirelle Tideglass when the party needs a socially grounded face for Hullwake Foundry: access, warning, delay, price, or reluctant help.
private_contradiction: They want to appear consistent, but their real choices are split between duty, self-protection, and preserving one relationship they cannot afford to lose.
